Our family has been part of the Clementi Woods Kindergarten (CWK) community for several years, and we couldn’t be more grateful for the experience.

From day one, CWK has felt like a second home — a place where our child has been cared for and encouraged.  Every child is welcomed by name, from the morning greetings at the gate to the cheerful assembly and into the classroom. It’s something that truly touched us — knowing that not just our child, but every student is known personally by the staff.

The teachers, many of whom have been with the school for over a decade, bring not only experience but a deep love for what they do. They take time to understand each child, helping them grow in confidence, independence, and character.

What sets CWK apart is its strong foundation in faith and values. The principles of love, care, sharing, giving, and growing aren’t just taught — they’re lived out daily in the classroom and playground. The admin team, led by the principal, is warm, responsive, and always open to feedback. We’ve felt heard and supported every step of the way.

One of our child’s favourite parts of the day has been the morning assembly — filled with praise and worship songs, laughter, and energy. It’s a joyful start that sets the tone for learning and connection.

CWK may not have all the bells and whistles of a commercial preschool, but it offers something far more valuable: a safe, consistent, and faith grounded environment where children thrive.

We’re so thankful for the strong foundation it’s given our child — and for the community we’ve found here.

A fairly good kindergarten with kind and helpful staff. The admin team is really good and accommodating.

However, the start of each day at CWK is overstimulating for a preschooler as loud music will be blasted at assembly - I supposed the school wants to hype up the entire atmosphere for kids but a more calming start might be an easier transition for kids.

Some teaching staff have been around for more than 15 years so some of their methods, though well-intentioned, might be less attuned to the needs of current generation of kids and borders on being disempowering for children who need a little more support. There is a lot of focus on traditional teaching methods like worksheets and less hands-on activities that promotes experiential learning and creative thinking.

School bus driver is a little eccentric and would pick a bone with parents who enter the school compound earlier (understandably, cars can only enter at 2.20pm due to limited space available) - even if permission has been sought to enter the school (happened to me twice, and I’ve witnessed this happen to other parents several times too).

I will recommend this school for a faith-based environment, super nice/easy to work with admin team (principal, admin, etc). However, if you’re looking for teachers who are nurturing, patient, supportive, and updated in their teaching methods, it might be less than desirable. The school is big on conformity and very unsupportive of independent thinking.

We have switched our child from this school to another school and found that we really prefer a quieter/ calming start to the day and we find it more empowering for our preschooler to be in the company of teachers who understand the need for hands-on learning, are patient and more nurturing. We find our child now more confident and less scared to express her views in class. It’s regretful that our child did not have a positive experience with her teachers at CWK as she often talks about her fear of being scolded. This has a lasting impact on her that we are still trying to work on how. It’s very regretful and one of the poorest choices I have made.

This is a personal and honest view from my end, with due respect given to the school and their staff. Written with the sole intent for other parents to understand the environment before they dive in as quite a lot of energy, time and money will be involved if you choose to enrol in any school of your choice.

This is the best kindergarten you can send your child to. All my four children attend this kindergarten.

It is not expensive. They have a holistic program that expose your child to various subjects.

They teach your children life skills , math and also Chinese and handicraft.

The Chinese teacher speaks to your child only in mandarin, which helps stem learn the language.

The children love going to school and it gives them such a good impression of school, starting them on the right track to  learning.

If you are interested in sending your child here, you need to queue up early to register because it is extremely popular.

Furthermore, the uniform looks great  😃

I am so grateful for what CWK has done for my kids. They acquired discipline, maintained healthy habits and happiness, while excelled academically. The teachers are loving and supportive. I can't ask for more.

The pastoral aspect is very good. During HBL, we appreciated the effort to create engaging in-house videos and worksheets/ workbooks.

Edited:
As many private kindergartens no longer operate because of the changing landscape and costing, I hope to express our family's gratitude. CWK has taught my child self-discipline, a desire to do one's best and to behave with integrity. Not that it really matters, but of the 6 children who went into her primary school, 4 are in the stretch classes.

For us, it was not so much the quantity of content but the nurturing of the character that made the difference. Reflective learning , being metacognitive, has made her purposeful.

My child is proud to say she was a CWK kid and 5 years after K2, she tells me she wishes she could re-live her Kindy years.

We’ve been so happy with our girls’ experience at LFP West Coast! The teachers put a lot of thought and effort into creating engaging programmes that the children truly enjoy. Every day, our girls come home excited to share what they’ve learned. It’s clear they feel confident and happy in school.

We also really appreciate the school’s strong parent engagement with regular updates, thoughtful communication, and opportunities to be part of the learning journey. The fees are reasonable for the quality of care and education provided, making it great value for money.

Highly recommend the school to any parent looking for a nurturing, enriching environment where children can thrive!

Both my boys are enrolled in LFP@West Coast. Teachers are friendly and dedicated in their roles, many times going over and above. Kids enjoy school alot. Curriculum is good and my p1 going kid has transited well to primary school. Highly recommended!

We are so grateful to have found this childcare centre! All my 4 kids are with this preschool! The teachers are incredibly caring, patient and professional — they treat every child like their own and always create a warm, nurturing environment. Our child has grown so much in confidence, independence and language ever since joining. The activities are creative and well-structured, and we love the regular photo updates that let us see what our little one is learning each day. The centre is clean, safe and very well-organised. Highly recommend to any parent looking for a loving and supportive place for their child to grow and thrive! ❤️

Due to the closing of the previous preschool last year, I frantically needed to find a new school for my son. Was recommended to this school which is not too far away from home.

Boy was happy since day 1. Even though we will only be here for 1 year, he has grown more mature and more responsible and look forward to going to school every day. I am sure the teachers and staff (and the kids!) have welcomed him with open arms.

Thank you to Mrs Teoh, the teachers and staff for making his final year of preschool memorable.

P.S. Even though I am only familiar with his form teachers, the other teachers also shared with me what they observed and feedback to me during pick up. Appreciate it!

My daughter has just joined PG early this year (at 25 months), and the teachers have been so patient with her - to get her comfortable, to correct her actions and helped her integrate into the class.

She has been enjoying going to school! She communicates better, eat well and get to socialize more with her other peers and teachers.

Happy to have gotten off the waitlist and have Little Footprints as my daughter’s first school

I am thrilled to share my positive experience with ICS School, where my child has been a student for the past two years. The school's commitment to biblical teachings is truly praiseworthy. Students actively engage in Bible memorization, prayer, and weekly worship, fostering a profound connection with God from an early age.

The teachers at ICS School deserve special commendation. Their warmth and patience stand out, empowering students to build confidence and cultivate politeness. The well-maintained teacher-to-student ratio ensures personalized attention, contributing to a nurturing learning environment.

Beyond academics, I appreciate the school's efforts in organizing various engaging activities that strengthen the connection between parents, teachers, and students. These initiatives create a vibrant community and enhance the overall educational experience.

In summary, I am grateful for the positive impact ICS School has had on my child's spiritual and academic growth. The dedication to character development, coupled with a supportive community, makes ICS School an exceptional choice for families seeking a well-rounded education.

I am a parent of two children, one in middle school and another in elementary school, both attending ICS (International Community School). Moving from India to Singapore, we were searching for a school that would be a blessing for our family, and we are incredibly grateful to have found ICS.

The school follows the American curriculum, and we couldn't be happier with how it has nurtured our children not only academically but also as individuals with a serving heart. The teachers at ICS are exceptional, creating countless opportunities for experiential learning and fostering critical thinking, creativity, communication skills, and teamwork.

As a family, we have been impressed by the school's commitment to providing an international exposure and academic growth. The holistic educational experience offered by ICS has truly exceeded our expectations.

We wholeheartedly recommend ICS to all families seeking a school that not only offers a top-notch education but also prioritizes the overall development of students. Moving to Singapore and finding this school has been a true blessing for us.

A big thank you to the entire ICS community for providing our children with an enriching and supportive learning environment!

Out of all the international schools that I've visited, ICS was the best suited for my child. It had the right balance of academic rigor, pastoral care and just great vibes from everyone that we met. My child continues to flourish because of the genuine and intentional interest and care from teachers and staff.

The most genuine people here, a very safe place and the children feel loved. It’s where all the children are seen and known—really cared for. The staff knows the children on a first name basis. Thank you ICS!!

We are so thankful for our time at ICS. It has been such a blessing to be part of a school that is truly rooted in Christian values. The education my kid received was not only academically solid but also grounded in faith, character, and love for others.

What stood out most to us was the dedication of the teachers and staff. They pour their hearts into the students, and that heart of service has made a lasting impression on our family. The ICS community is small, warm, and close-knit, and my kids made wonderful friendships that we know will stay with them for a life time. We are especially grateful for the wonderful Parent Teacher Fellowship and the Moms in Prayer team, who faithfully support the school and community with love, encouragement, and prayer. Their presence is a beautiful reflection of the Christ-centered spirit that makes ICS feel like a second home.

Highly recommend this school to any family looking for strong academics, caring community, and a Christ-centered education.

When I Have A Short Walk To Tuas, I Went To This Lighthouse For A Meanwhile. I Just Watch The River / Sea. The Sunset View Is Fantastic. I Enjoyed To This Lighthouse. This Lighthouse Is Very Important To Visit When You're In The Tuas.

Raffles Marina Lighthouse is a beautiful and peaceful spot, perfect for sunset views. The lighthouse itself is small but charming, surrounded by yachts, open sea views, and a calm marina atmosphere.
The sea breeze and lovely scenery make it a relaxing place to unwind. It’s especially popular for photography during golden hour and offers a nice escape from the busy city.

Visitors need to register at the guardhouse before entering. It’s a perfect place for a quiet walk, catching the sunset, and enjoying a relaxing stroll.

It's an amazing place to see the sunset at the beachside. It's located at the corner of Singapore, beside Tuas Link MRT, just a walkable distance away. The public is allowed at all times for free, and the place is quite calm and cool, whereas weekends will be a bit crowded, but not much, due to its location.

You'll get an awesome view of the sunset, as you can see from the photos included with this review. There are no shops for food and beverages nearby, so it's better to bring your own if you want to spend time there. You can see people fishing as a hobby. At the end of the bridge, there is a non-operational lighthouse, which is best for photography. You can typically spend about 2 to 3 hours here, whether you are alone or with friends, as this place has a nice view.

Note: There is restaurant at this place but it's too expensive.

Enjoy your visit.

A calm and beautifully maintained lighthouse with a peaceful coastal vibe. Visiting during sunset is especially rewarding—the sky colors, gentle sea breeze, and the soft glow of the lighthouse create a very soothing atmosphere. The walkway is clean and well lit, making it perfect for a relaxed evening stroll or quiet photography. You can hear the water, feel the calm, and momentarily disconnect from city noise. Not overly crowded, which adds to its charm. A hidden gem in Singapore for those who enjoy serene waterfront views and simple moments.

First time exploring Tuas Link mrt and the Light House was recommended.
It's a short walk from Tuas Link mrt. Turn left upon exit and then turn another left into Raffles Marina which is a beautiful place.

The stroll to the Light House is free. Although the Light House was a little small, the walk was relaxing with ocean breeze. However it's not sheltered so maybe useful to bring a waterproof jacket or raincoat, depending on the weather. Wind was too strong to use umbrella on a rainy day.

There's Marina bistro nearby for fish & chip and Captains Table for Chinese food upstairs etc. Prices are not cheap. Also has swimming pool and jacuzzi etc (for marina members I think).
Awesome location if you wana shoot cars or any rooftop parking lot related scenes. Toilets are clean too and good holding area for make-up and crew meals
Big building which have few blocks. May be difficult for newcomers to search for the unit as the lift was not connected to all units.

Internet, chatgbt, google, Ai and the pervasive mobile devices may have changed the way we seek answers. But a library is where serious learnings and discoveries are found.

They are depository of knowledge, curated and preserved. The Central Library is that - and more.

There are librarians dedicated to the preservation of knowledge and their proliferation. Here, they are not stuffy and grumpy matrons. They are professionals and helpful in a way we boomers never knew.

Tell them a hypothesis and they can help you turn an idea into blueprint. Need to fill a gap in citations, they can find you a landfill.

History is our heritage. Lessons learned to inform us. Latest technology is flashy but libraries tell us how they came about.

The Central Library is more than a meeting place for varsity students. Here is where a thousand nuggets of wisdom and knowledge exists - waiting for those with inquiring minds to be transformed into “professors.”

lots of study spots! most of the library is for nus students/staff only as there are gantries at the entrance which mainly open with student/staff passes

most study spots have charging spots, each level has a bathroom and water dispensers. there's also food options at level 1 and a bookstore with essentials

only complaint is that the wifi can be quite slow/spotty at times. also level 6 study area can get quite warm sometimes when its busy, like the ac isnt strong enough :")))

I spend a lot of time in this library when I did my undergrad and post grad studies. It has many books which were not available in other libraries. I did join as a corporate member for a few years too.
Beautiful and modern library with a very rich collection of books and ample seating space.

I entered as an alumnus. I needed an NUS alumnus lifelong email address and also had to download and install the UNivUS app, which generates the QR code for me to enter the library. There was mobile data signal at the registration counter therefore I needed to log into NUG Guest wifi. But the NUS Guest wifi needed an OTP which required mobile signal! Therefore I needed to go back and forth between the Forum (mobile signal to get OTP) and library registration desk (for wifi). Altogether, it took me perhaps half an hour to sort it a out and finally enter the library. Future trips should be straightforward I hope!!! General stacks on 5th floor. Photocopier is on 5th floor but in the Chinese SEA Collection. Use your EZ-link card. $0.03 per A4 - great value!  :-)  The facilities are lovely -wow... compared to 1990s! Love the place. The Coop had lots of cute merch too. Ah... to be a Gen Z or Gen Alpha... 😄😄😄

Since moving back to the west from the east, Sheng Siong has become one of my usual supermarkets that I frequently get my groceries from. The supermarket is spacious with a wide variety of selections, and everything is always neatly arranged. The vegetables and fruits are fresh as well.

The staff there are also very friendly, especially two ladies whom I encountered.  Darchana and Santos are very helpful and professional. Darchana always smiley and very helpful whenever assistance is needed. Small gestures like these really make the shopping experience more comfortable and pleasant.

I was in a rush so I didn’t manage to take more pictures, but I’ll update with more photos the next round.

Cashiers are fast and friendly. Will remind customers if they are Chas or Pioneer on the designated days.

The 2 storey Sheng Siong is 24 hours. Appreciate the long operating hours.

Peaceful shopping can be done after mid night.

Early in the morning, there's staff to help butcher pork to ur desire. Choose a piece of meat and they minced for u. Pork Belly if not much people, they can slice it for u.

Fish counters operate till later timing then pork. Large selection of prawns, fish, crabs.

Outdoor area, there's plant section, veg section and promotion section.

Variety is huge!

Efficient, tidy, with enormous range of food, groceries, household appliances and sanitary products that you might not find in normal grocery stores.

The clean and well lit enterprise also keeps jaywalkers and suspicious people away, there used to be provocatively dressed women harassing motorcyclists for cigarettes years ago. They and an ever rising count of shoplifters have been dealt with swiftly by the authorities.

It helps that the staff are very friendly and welcoming to all cultures and preserve the Singapore spirit of orderly and honest business.

24-hour supermarket at West with a wide range of food & many varieties. Never worry about being unable to buy fresh seafood cod this outlet 24 hours yet can find almost everything we need. From food to household items, too

I love supermarkets, especially the bigger ones. I miss the old Sheng Siong Supermarket at Tekka Mall, which had unfortunately closed years ago.
This outlet seems rather big to me; Spacious with a great selection of affordable daily essentials. A really good place for grocery shopping.

** 4% special discount for seniors (Singaporeans/ PRs) 60 and above, every Tuesday and Wednesday.
**The Tem Tem Protein Tempeh Chips sold here are suitable for vegetarians, healthy and super delicious. Get yours now for the coming Chinese New Year. You’re worth pampering.

send her for customer service course fast.

To the Owner reading this,
Your Shop is fine, reasonable pricing, convenient to buy daily items. But your cashier is killing your business, collecting parcel also bring foot fall to your shop too.

When her work attitude and behaviour improves, so will your ratings.

Hope  to see cashier improvement soon.

Very very bad service by the cashier, it’s a Vietnamese. First and foremost I’ve got no issues with foreigners, but I’m really fed up with her service. Bought a total of 13 coca colas and asked her if there was any bulk discounts, but she got fed-up instead and rejected me rudely, and I just asked for the whole price and she was fed up and kept sighing here and there like we’re like cheapskate customers. It’s only just asking about the price and I’m a paying customer here. Could have tell me politely. What’s wrong with her? Please take a good look at the photos

Horrible service. When I got my items and approached the cashier, she didn't acknowledge me! She actually continued fixing her parcels for a good few mins while I was just standing there🤯 should i have just left? Would that be doing her a favour? Will not be back even tho I live just opp. Also, her parcels were all over the counter, customers can't even put their purchases down. Joke.

Looks like they're too busy with their parcel collection side biz, to scan ur items for payment🤡

Quite a big supermarket located at a new public housing project. The supermarket is located right below the multi storey carpark, facing the main road. The supermarket was pretty well stocked up when I was there. Reasonable pricing.

If you’ve ever wondered what it’s like to be treated like an inconvenience for daring to be a customer, look no further than this charming little convenience stall near my place. Based on several recent reviews, I see I'm not alone in experiencing the warm hospitality of their cashier – a Malaysian aunty with bleached blonde hair tied up in a ponytail and a permanent expression that screams, “You owe me rent.”

She greets every paying customer like it’s the worst part of her day (and possibly her life), and I've personally witnessed her tossing out curt, borderline rude responses to people just trying to… you know, pay for things. One of my neighbors – a poor soul – even shared that when he politely suggested she be a little more courteous, she shouted back at him. Now that’s customer engagement, isn't it?

The best part? There’s little to no info available about who runs this place, so if you thought you might want to raise a complaint or even just leave feedback – good luck! But hey, who needs accountability when you’ve got that level of charm behind the counter?

Prices aren’t particularly great either, but if you’re in the West Coast Park area and feel like ruining a perfectly good day with a side of attitude and a splash of rudeness, this place is an absolute must-visit!

They cut and pack sprouted onions for sale.

I've been training with Theo at Level Up Gym in One-North for more than a month now, and it's been a really good experience. The gym is clean, well-equipped, and never too crowded—perfect for focused sessions.

Theo brings a wealth of experience, is always very encouraging and motivates you when you feel like giving up. What I’ve appreciated most is his attention to form and injury prevention. I came in with a history of lower back injury, and Theo tailored my program to gradually build strength and confidence without pushing recklessly.

Highly recommend if you want smart, no-BS training with a coach who knows his stuff.

While is Singapore, Lloyd was my PT & he transformed the way I looked after my body & increased my confidence in using weights.  Lloyd’s expertise, attention to detail, tailored programme & strong communication (in addition to our enjoyable conversations) made me look forward to our sessions.  Now back in the UK I miss my PT sessions with Lloyd but we keep in regular contact.  I definitely recommend you consider Level Up… you don’t know what you’re missing 😁💪

I began my fitness journey with Coach Matthias at Level Up Gym in January 2026, and it has been an incredible experience.

His patience, expertise, and attention to detail - especially in ensuring proper form, helped me grow stronger and build confidence in my training.

I’m truly grateful to have him as my coach and would highly recommend him to anyone looking for guidance on their fitness journey.

I trained with Lloyd prior to my wedding and all I can say is that he is FANTASTIC! Lloyd is super knowledgeable, extremely personable and I hit all my goals in record time training with him. The gym space is high end, discreet and private. Overall Level Up and Lloyd is the premier combination in Singapore to smash your fitness goals and work on injury recovery! Thank you Lloyd!

My partner and I joined Level Up Gym to get back into exercising after quite a long break, and Coach Matthias Tan has been amazing from day one. He’s super attentive, patient, and always makes sure we’re doing each movement safely and correctly.

What we really appreciate is how open he is to feedback and how he customizes our workouts to match our fitness goals. Every session feels fun, motivating, and just the right challenge for us.

If you’re looking for a supportive place to restart your fitness journey, I can’t recommend Level Up Gym and Coach Matthias enough! 💪

Foundation member since 2023 Feb. Been coming here almost daily. Chill place with very supportive coaches. Shout out to Dalston, the best coach ever. Also special mention to Kimmy Erul and Cheryl for their guidance.

Joined BFT in July 2025. Fitness has improved! Great coaches, especially Coach Chris as he saw I had challenges from years of no exercise, always patient in correcting my form to ensure I get maximum benefit with minimum stress to the body!!

Seems that the place lacks privacy. Whole gym can be seen from outside. Very small area. Classes seems to be conducted with booking sessions only.

Place looks clean and is always well packed at the end of the day.
Great team here at BFT WCP. Community feel, fun sessions and great trainers genuinely interested in supporting you to achieve your goals.

Coaches are friendly! They will do some explanation before the workout start. The space not so spacious but also the class capacity is small.  I have improve alot after I joined few months! No shower room inside but is walkable distance from my house. I going to BFT almost everyday as it consists of cardio and strength! I always have Fun workout and community is nice!

Forest Ramble is a fantastic children’s play wonderland nestled in nature. The themed play zones — Monkey Play, Squirrel Play, and Snake Play — are creatively designed and encourage lots of climbing, balancing, and imaginative play. It feels adventurous yet thoughtfully integrated into the surrounding greenery

The playground is well laid out with different zones catering to kids of various ages, making it easy for children to explore while adults supervise comfortably. Do note that some sections may be under maintenance at times, but there’s still plenty to keep kids entertained
A beautiful relaxing park and with nice playground. Except that some parts are closed for maintenance since 7th July 2025. That’s quite a long time for maintenance….

One of the most exciting children outdoor playground.
Plenty of different play stations.
Swings, spring jumps, slides, flying fox, mini trampoline, adventure bridges, rope runs, see saws and many many more.
Exciting moments for kids age 5 to 12 years old and 2 to 5 yrs old with adult supervision.
Shaded area, water cooler, drinks vending machine and toilets are available.
Opening hrs:
8:00am - 10:00pm,
Closed on Mon (except for PH) and Tue if preceding Mon is a PH

This was a great place to bring my nephews and cousins. The playground is spacious, fun, and has a lot of different areas to explore, so it kept everyone entertained. It’s well designed, safe, and a really nice outdoor spot for families to spend time together.

Very nice playground with lot of play setup for kids from swings to slides. Jumping climbing everything was there in the playground. On Rainy days play equipments are not allowed to use until dry. Totally recommended

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

If you love nature, I recommend you doing a hike here. It’s good for beginners and all the trails are colour coded for the difficulty level. Along the trails, there will be sheltered post for you to rest. You can easily spot some common dwellers such as snakes, monkeys and wild boar. If you are lucky enough, you may find some cool birds. I come here almost every week and spend 1-2hrs doing the hike.

Follow the trail to Jelutong Tower and Tree Top Walk. Enjoy the view and the trail. Have not cover all the trails. Will be back to try and experience other trails. Good experience to spend weekends with nature. Escape from the city view.

This area consist of a network of jungle trails where you can trek. There many tracks that you can walk and the natural forest canopy provides a wonderful refreshing air with minimal pollution. Many kind of flora that you can enjoy along the way. You can track anyway between 45 mins to over 4 hours.

The place is serene! Most of the paths are manmade now so it's easier to hike up to tree top walk! There are A LOT of monkeys do keep your loose items safe at all times, also multiple signs are put up to not feed the monkeys.

speechlessly beautiful. just yeah. the biggest nature reserve in sg.

ps there’s a lot of monkeys so pls don’t bring plastic bags, they’re very aggressive as well
Nice place for walking and jogging. The trails are well marked. Great nature!!!

As a nearby resident, I visit Jurong Lake Gardens quite frequently. The walking tracks are always well-maintained, and I enjoy the scenic lake views. There are also plenty of shaded paths, which make for a very relaxing walk.

A pleasant but fairly small section of Jurong Lake Gardens. The landscaping is neat and well maintained, and there are some nice plants and boardwalk paths to stroll through, but it doesn’t take very long to cover the entire area

It’s fine to visit if you’re already nearby or doing a full loop of the gardens, but probably not worth going out of your way just for this section alone

The newly renovated Jurong Park @Chinese Garden @Japanese Garden is amazing. Many interesting plants, ponds, and fixtures are unique to only this park. Many themes are nicely decorated.

Food stores are scattered everywhere while facilities like toilets and water dispensers are generously available.

It's a good park for exploration, evening walks, and slow biking.

A huge garden! Took about 2 hours to slowly stroll, enjoy the view and reach the other end of the garden. It’s home to many animals and there’s so much to see. My favorite starts from the Japanese Garden onwards. Really enjoy the peaceful environment there and nice place to totally unwind. It’s a short walking distance from the MRT station.
nice and peaceful place. great for walks, jogs and runs. some do cycle around.

The Central Beach Bazaar comprises:

1. The Wings of Time fireworks-cum-musical fountain
2. A row of food kiosks selling finger foods
3. A white building containing "Good Old Days" food court/restaurant and Sentosa souvenir shop

Wings of Time
===
Singapore's award-winning and only nightly fireworks extravaganza featuring a narrative about Shahbaz, a pre-historic bird and his friends. It's a multi-sensory night show set against the open sea, immense in the firework effects, dazzling fountains, laser and fire effects.

Great way to end the day at Sentosa with Beach monorail station 100m away.

Duration - It's only 20min, which is actually just about right for a musical fountain performance.

The seats are outdoor, so it's weather-dependent.

Free seating - so go early (half hour) to get a good seat and enjoy the sea breeze. Seats up to 6th and 7th row can get wet!

$19 ticket if u buy from operator directly and in-person.
$13 if you buy via from online travel/ticket merchants such as Klook.

Good Old Days
====

For months in 2025, this was the only "affordable" dining place in Sentosa after the Malaysian Food Street at Resorts World closed down in Feb 2025 after 13 yrs. A new food court with 10 stalls (Feast Food Hall at Weave) has opened since Oct 2025 but awaits to be reviewed, although initial look shows higher food prices.

Anyway, for Good Old Days, mains start from $9.80:

Nasi Lemak Chicken/ Fish ($9.80)

+ Has cashless payment

Nearest toilets are at the Beach Station, or base of Skyline Luge, or inside the AltitudeX building (where Shake Shack and Marrybrown are located).

the service crew name lucas and chef affande was very helpful and kind to explain each of the tacos fillings and which are the most recommended flavours.
they are very nice and friendly and even would have start some small talks while i was waiting for my food. price wise was also reasonable as the combos are of value which includes a taco, chips and a drink for just $12.80

Nice and affordable food! Friendly and helpful staff! Love it when we can purchase snacks (the fish and chips is great!) from this burger stall and spend the evening at the beach before Wings of Time show.

My favourite fish and chips in Singapore, surprisingly found at a hotdog and burger window store in Sentosa! Perfectly fried, super tasty, and reasonably priced despite the touristy location. The service was fantastic too, which really added to the full beachside food experience. The family wiped the food out before I could even take a photo. Ended up grabbing some corn dogs to go, just to complete the hotdog-by-the-beach vibe.
the environment is very inviting and the cashiers are very friendly and helpful in explaining the menu at taco pau and charcoal pit

Sentosa is a beautiful island with a lot to see and do. Clean, well-maintained, and very family-friendly. Great beaches, fun attractions, and a relaxing vibe. Loved the mix of nature and entertainment — perfect for a day out in Singapore!

Beautiful place to unwind and enjoy the ocean. The beach is clean, the atmosphere is relaxing, and there’s a good balance between lively areas and quieter spots. It’s great whether you want to walk along the shore, sit back and relax, or just enjoy the scenery for a few hours. One of the nicest beach escapes in Singapore.

Exceptional Experience at Sentosa Beach

Sentosa Beach is a remarkable destination that truly stands out for its pristine environment and vibrant atmosphere. The beach boasts impeccably clean, soft sand, making it a pleasure to walk along and relax.

The crowd is well-mannered and adds to the positive vibe of the area, ensuring a pleasant and enjoyable experience for visitors of all ages. The facilities are well-maintained, and the surrounding amenities enhance the overall convenience and appeal of the beach.

Whether you’re seeking a peaceful retreat or a lively spot to unwind, Sentosa Beach offers the perfect blend of tranquility and energy. It’s a must-visit for anyone looking to experience the best of Singapore’s coastal charm.
Beautiful beach n clean🥰
Beautiful beach safe for children to play and great for beginners paddle boarding

The Northern Tuas Basin is the basin of water bound by Tuas Road to the East. In URA Masterplan 2019, it is marked as soon to be used for Business 2 (Industrial) use. The basin is currently used by industrial waterfront companies for mooring and transit.
Good
Nice and friendly area

place_name: Star Learners @ Yung Ho | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
Both of my children have been with Star Learners Yung Ho for the past 6 years, starting from the PG till K2 graduation class. It has been a truly wonderful experience. The centre is excellent and responsive, and I’ve always felt that my children are in very good hands.

The principal, Ms Lois and all Star Learners Yung Ho teachers are extremely patient, kind, and gentle with the kids. They were always reassuring and consistently showed great dedication in caring and guiding the children. The centre has positive and nurturing vibe and I’m truly grateful that my children were able to grow up in such a holistic and supportive environment, leaving a lasting impression on their early years.

place_name: Star Learners @ Yung Ho | Preschool & Childcare
review_text:
We enrolled our child here from infant care all the way to K1, and it has been a wonderful journey with the school.

The teachers are caring, attentive and genuinely passionate about what they do. We really appreciate that their teaching approach is literature-based, which helps the children relate better to what they are learning and makes lessons more engaging and meaningful.

The environment is always clean, safe and spacious, giving the kids plenty of room to explore and move around comfortably.

Thank you to the team for creating such a nurturing and enriching experience for our child! 😊

place_name: Star Learners @ Yung Ho | Preschool & Childcare
review_text: A fantastic school with a heart! The teachers are professional and warm, and the curriculum is full of fun learning journeys and hands-on exploration. My child is always excited to go to school and comes home full of stories about the interesting things they discovered that day.

place_name: Star Learners @ Yung Ho | Preschool & Childcare
review_text: It has been a wonderful journey partnering with the principal and teachers from SL@YH! My boy started as a transfer student in N1 and despite long settling period, the teachers were patient and assuring. Ms Lois, the principal, is a experienced principal who is warmth and approachable. I would like to shout out to Tr Jaime, Zhang Laoshi and Tr Haoting! Thank you for everything and he is now finally graduated from K2!

place_name: Star Learners @ Yung Ho | Preschool & Childcare
review_text: The teachers and staff are patient and caring. The private playground and the large atrium space for riders are a huge plus for the children to get outdoor and physical activity. The enrichment lessons are also varied and of high quality. My child attended Star Leaners @ Yung Ho for over 4 years and loved every moment of it!

Both my kids have been to this preschool — my elder joined at K2 and my youngest started in nursery. From day one, the principal and teachers have been so caring and patient. I’ll never forget how the principal personally comforted my crying youngest on her first day and called me less than an hour later to say she was already happily playing.

The teachers are warm, approachable, and truly love the children. The school has a fun and engaging curriculum with phonics, STEAM activities, play gym, and even spelling to prepare for primary school. My kids also love the many English and Chinese nursery songs they learn here.

I also really appreciate that the school listens to parents’ feedback and suggestions — it shows how much they value working together for the children. My kids always look forward to school, and even my elder still misses it now in primary school. Highly recommend!

A few years ago, I made the decision to transfer my two boys from the school across the road and enroll them in David & Goliath Preschool. Though it’s no longer within walking distance, I’ve never once looked back.

Now, with my older son moving on to Primary 3 and my younger one graduating from D&G, I feel deeply blessed that they have been nurtured and taught by the dedicated teachers here.

As believers, we are especially grateful for the strong spiritual foundation that D&G has laid—one upon which our children can continue to build their faith and walk with God. For families who are not Christians, this preschool still instills strong values that equip children to thrive in today’s complex, and at times, troubled world.

Thank you, David & Goliath Preschool, for shining the light of God’s glory and planting mustard seeds of faith in the hearts of these little ones.

David Goliath preschool stands out as a warm and caring environment where my child is nurtured with patience, kindness, and respect. The principal and the teachers are attentive, patient, kind and supportive. I see how well my child has grown. Values such as empathy, responsibility, and curiosity are instilled through daily activities, helping children to build both confidence and character. I always see happy children enjoying every session. My child is always excited to share the fruitful day with us.

I'm incredibly surprised they are a Christian preschool. The Chinese teacher did not display any situation awareness, nor kindness when she rudely took her entire class and cut infront of my kids who were having their photo taken at the banner board. She even asked us to move away!

Their teachers need to LEAD by example, and not just muscle into their getting their way.

Wonderful experience with David & Goliath Preschool. The staff is a great mix of teachers from diverse backgrounds, ages, and levels of experience, which brings a rich and balanced approach to education. They are incredibly caring, kind, thoughtful, and they do an excellent job of instilling both valuable skills and positive habits in the children.

What we especially appreciate is the school’s balanced focus on both social-emotional developments and academic foundations. It’s been the perfect environment for both of my kids, and I’m glad we chose this school for their early years.

Teachers here are very nurturing and caring, as well as experienced. Our son joined the PG class and has grown so much both in his content learning as well as social development. He used to be timid, but now is able to socialize well, have fun with his friends and follow instructions in a group context well. All thanks to teachers facilitating his transition! 100% will recommend little footprints lakeside. Special shoutout to Ms Hana, Teacher Sue, and Jia Laoshi who have been so wonderful.

Ome of the Little Footprints preschool in Jurong located opposite of Lakeside Primary school.. Easy access to the main Corporation road and just opposite bus stop.. Kids enjoy study here. Friendly Teacher and  big school compound.

My 18-month-old loves going to school and has settled in really well, and that’s entirely down to the love and care from his wonderful teachers. They’ve gone out of their way to really get to know him as a child, and I loved watching him form warm, caring relationships so quickly. He always comes home happy.
The principal, Ms Dharshini, is also incredibly supportive and caring — of both students and parents. Highly recommend!

My daughter Alessandra has really enjoyed her classes at Little Footprint Lakeside! She loves her teachers who are patient and fun. She enjoys the variety of activities the teachers thoughtfully created and led the children through. Many Thanks to  Ms Farhana, Xiao Lao Shi and all the teachers that take care of her! We really appreciate your care and support for our daughter’s growth! 🌹🌹🌹

We love how warm and caring everyone is at Little Footprints @ Lakeside. The teachers are super friendly and really make our child feel at home. There’s always something fun going on, like excursions and family events that helps us all feel more connected.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text: Our child transferred from China to K2 at Raffles Kidz Pres. Thanks to all teachers’ patient care, especially Chinese teacher Wen, he adapted fast and enjoys school life now. We are satisfied with the school’s warm environment and teaching.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Our son has been in school for 1.5yrs since he was 18mo. During transition from PN to Nursery was when we were given feedback on his development, and at which point we considered changing schools for something that was more suitable for him. Principal Gwen stepped in and offered a change in class and teachers and we agreed to see if this allowed our son to enjoy school and learn better. We are so glad we were encouraged to this step because fast forward 6m later, our son has shown so much growth in social, emotional, physical & cognitive development. We must especially commend Teacher Arinah and Xing Lao Shi, his form teachers, to whom both have been very dedicated in caring and guiding our son along the way. Particularly, there was more feedback provided to us either by phone calls or after school drop off chats — that helped us to also support the teachers work in helping our son’s progress. Our son has since been much happier in going and talking about school.

We would also like to thank Teacher Adillah, Zann and Anna who also made school so much more enjoyable with their warmth and investment to really engaging (chatting) with our boy!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text: The teachers are always so welcoming during drop-off and pick-up which gives parents great peace of mind. We transferred our son here and it has definitely been the right choice. The teachers have been patient with him especially during meal times and we’ve seen great progress that he is now toilet trained. Lessons are also well rounded and engaging. Shoutout to Teacher Zann and Xing 老师 for their dedication and care.

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
Raffles Kidz has been an amazing preschool experience for our daughter. From the very first day, the teachers made her feel incredibly warm, welcomed, and safe. You can really feel their dedication — they take the time to understand each child’s personality, and they create an environment where the kids are excited to learn and explore.
Our daughter looks forward to school every morning, and we’ve seen her grow so much in confidence, language, and social skills. The curriculum is engaging, the activities are thoughtfully planned, and the entire team communicates well with parents.
We’re truly grateful to the teachers and staff at Raffles Kidz for giving our little one such a happy and nurturing start to her learning journey. Highly recommended for any parent looking for a caring and enriching preschool!

place_name: Raffles Kidz @ Jurong West (Yuan Ching) - Best Childcare and Preschool Singapore
review_text:
My kids have attended Raffles Kidz since Pre-Nursery and I can say with confidence, “No regrets at all!”

The school compound is spacious, neat, safe and definitely a fun learning environment for the kids to be in. The curriculum is enriching and holistic with plenty of engaging activities that supports both academic and social development. Stars of the centre have to be the teachers and principal! They are patient, kind and encouraging towards the students.

Special mention to Tr Triff, 牛老师, Tr Rosz and 张老师!

Tr Triff and Niu Laoshi have played a significant role in my boy’s early education journey, building a strong foundation for my son as he graduates from K2 this year. I’m truly grateful for their commitment in nurturing my boy, helping him reach his full potential and preparing him well for primary school.

I also deeply appreciate Zhang Laoshi and Tr Rosz for their unwavering dedication and effort towards nurturing my daughter who is currently in N2. With their warmth, patience and guidance, my girl is able to learn and grow in a positive environment, and she has showed tremendous improvement in all aspects.

I definitely highly recommend RKI! For parents looking for a good pre-school, Raffles Kidz is your top choice.

A Dynaglass 20DX off Singapore Poly Marina with students onboard. This boat is the most popular boat for the practical lesson of MPA approved Powered Pleasure Craft Driving Licence (PPCDL) course.

Google MPA website for the "Introduction to Pleasure Craft Licensing" if you are keen to pursue PPCDL.

Some of the best times in my poly life we’re spent here. This is the only MPA approved PPCDL test center and is also home to SMTC global offshore training. Numerous Co-curricular clubs from the polytechnic also share this place, notably the SP sailing club. Classes are conducted here mainly for students of the Singapore Maritime Academy
No parking place, just road side parking. Power pleasure boat license practical test held here.
Couched in the corner beside RSYC is Poly Marina. If you are taking PPCDL your test is here.
STCW courses, PPCDL Course/Test Centre, Offshore Courses and etc.

Very clean and well maintained. I went there for a seminar on 30th May about Machining Learning and AI at NUS School of Computing.

The event was held at COM 1 building and was well organised by both NUS and EMERITUS.

Besides SOC, I visited NUH and NUS School of Design and Engineering as well and was impressed by its cleanliness.

Can’t wait to possibly start a postgraduate program here 😶‍🌫️

Had a great experience today at NUS Open House 2023! Although everything was only taking place at UTown, it was an extremely vibrant and enjoyable experience. There were so many activities and booths around, and it was such fun and pleasure to be at the place!

Thoroughly enjoyed my 1-day outing to the campus, where students and staff alike are very welcoming, friendly, and awesome! Thank you NUS :)

P.S. it’s a great time to visit on weekends just to walk around and admire the architecture of the campus!

Your application website is trash. I don't know who wrote your website but the person did not pass computer science for sure. Your website logs out every 30 minutes and the problem is you don't save the progress that students have made. In the end they have to refill everything again and you know how long it takes to do that. You better solve that asap otherwise you will lose  amazing students.

Amazing place for students worldwide. Top notch facilities for everything you care about. really interesting lot of students to hang around. You'll love this space , the Campus is attracted all over the city of Singapore. For vegetarians, it's a bit tricky to get the desired food. Amazing public transport and AQI less than 50.

I was amazed when I visited this campus back in 2014. Still I'm wondering how an university campus can be so beautiful. This campus is the combination of the any modern shopping mall (e.g. vibocity) and a decent forest. One can enjoyed these both feeling once he/she inside of it. Everything is heavily organized and the campus is so bug that you can not visit the entire campus in a single day. The campus is full of stairs and that's why sometimes it is called national university of Stairs.
Staff at mini mart dont have customer service skills. Staff show there are attitude. We may foreigner but we pay money for what we purchase. Pls educate the staff..specially who stand at cashier.
This is a nice market. And everything is very good
Good, dear frinds plz keep clean your tables after drinking, don't throw bear tins...  Save our environment

All materials price very high.

You buy other supermarkets $ 100 dollar compate this market you buy $ 120 dollar
Worsted behavior staffing
Good customer service and Excellent shop for all things.
Excellent shop
Price high

Kudos to the gym manager Ryan for being very bubbly and approachable although this is not my homegym. Highly recommend to drop by this gym if you're in the west or preparing for Hyrox. Although its abit further to travel to, it is worth it due to this gym being fully equipped with every Hyrox station and some less commonly found equipments such as the T-bar row which makes a pull/back day really complete. Additionally, it has a sandbag for kickboxing work which I really appreciate.

D'arena AF has a free charging shelf for your devices in the locker area, one small piece of towel provided, a very well ventilated spacious toilet and showers and nice overall layout.

10/10 will visit whenever I can 👍

I just signed up for a 24 month membership with Ryan and Colan, they provided a detailed tour of the gym and explained on the different equipments and their uses. Staff here are all so friendly and welcoming, making you feel like family instead of just a member coming to work out 😊

Definitely will recommend everyone to sign up here and join the D Arena AF family 😁

I have visited many different Anytime Fitness outlets, and this branch is definitely one of the best. The gym is very clean, well maintained, and the equipment is in excellent condition. The layout is spacious and well organized, making it comfortable to train even during busier hours.

The atmosphere is also very positive and welcoming. Staff are friendly and helpful, and the overall environment makes workouts enjoyable and motivating.

Great experience overall—highly recommend this outlet! 💪

Good outlet. Quite empty on a weekday afternoon. Plenty of squat racks and a platform for powerlifting. The variety machine is also great. The functional training area is big with good equipment such as sleds and ski-ergs. For those who love group exercises, they also have classes in the evenings. Staffs are quite friendly. They also provide small towels for your workout. Such a shame I don't stay anywhere near this outlet, else I would frequent here.

I’ve personally had a very good experience here. The gym environment is energetic, motivating, and always gives a positive vibe. The staff are friendly, helpful, and professional, which makes every visit comfortable and enjoyable.
The facilities are clean, equipment is well maintained, and the overall atmosphere really makes me look forward to coming back. The community here is also great and very welcoming.
For me, it’s a convenient and enjoyable place to work out. Highly recommended!

This is the main building of University Sports Centre, also known as USC. Located opposite Yusof Ishak House, this three-storey facility boasts features like swimming pools, squash courts, a gymnasium, and a rock-climbing wall. It’s also effectively a sheltered path for those travelling to Valour House from the direction of YIH.
Home of Team NUS. There are plenty of sports facilities here including swimming pool and squash courts.

The sports centre houses a variety of sports facilities, including two olympic-sized swimming pools (indoor and outdoor), gym, squash, among others.

The swimming pool is generally clean, and may get busy at times. I also particularly appreciate the clean changing rooms/toilet.
Well managed sports centre with vibrant community.
Sports complex for basketball, swimming, squash, table tennis etc. for NUS students and staff.

A really enjoyable stretch for a relaxed walk or a quiet break by the water. There are dedicated platforms here for fishing, and several spots to sit down and rest, making it a comfortable place to slow the pace and just take in the surroundings. It feels unhurried and well thought out, especially for people who want to linger rather than rush through

You can also walk all the way to the “End of Loop Plaza,” which makes this promenade a nice, satisfying segment of a longer stroll around the gardens. There’s a high chance of spotting impressive banyan trees and bits of wildlife along the way, adding to the sense of being immersed in nature

🦅🦋Southern Promenade is the quieter side of the Jurong Lake Gardens.
💨🌬️🌳Located at the southernmost end of the Garden, the lush Southern Promenade is majestically lined with the Rain trees and Banyan trees making the area comfortably shaded and it's atmosphere infused with a breath of fresh air.

Part of Jurong Lake Gardens compound. The section is situated at the Yuan Ching road side. There is no connecting path to the highway but only a loop. There are 3 promenades for chilling around (with no shelter), area is well-lit but get rather noisy from the traffic of the nearby highway.
I always cycle here. Very long and chill route I guess
Nice green for a retreat from the busy street.

Rifle Range Nature Park is a great extension to the Bukit Timah nature area, offering a mix of easy trails, lush rainforest scenery, and interesting glimpses into Singapore’s natural and historical heritage. The park is well connected, with clearly marked paths, boardwalks, and links to nearby nature reserves

One unexpected highlight was spotting a durian tree - and even seeing fallen durians on the ground, a reminder of how rich and diverse the forest ecosystem is. Along the trails, you’ll also find impressive giant trees, dense vegetation, quarry views, and remnants of the area’s past hidden among the greenery

I loved Rifle Range Nature Park — it’s very friendly for children and the elderly. The boardwalks are well-paved, anti-slip, and mostly barrier-free, so it’s easy for prams and seniors to walk comfortably.

The access is super convenient: just take Beauty World MRT Exit A, go to Level 4 of Beauty World Centre, and cross the overhead bridge that leads directly into the park. Highlights include the Quarry Wetland, Colugo Deck, and a fun Forest Exploration Trail for kids. Highly recommended for a relaxed family nature walk.

Nice new park with natural trails and paved paths. Has many unspoilt flora and fauna, saw several durian and starfruit trees along the paths. Not so easy to get to if depending on public transport. Toilets and carpark are available and some paths r equipped with CCTV! Not crowded on a weekday morning.

A nice trek away from the hustle and bustle of the city located in the Bukit Timah area of Singapore. Amidst a lush green rain forest with thriving ecosystems Easy to access and to reach on foot and public transport from the nearest MRT station called Beauty World - Downtown Line (Blue Coloured Line). An overhead bridge links one of the entrances of the park to the roof top Food Court (highest floor) of Beauty World Shopping Centre. Easy enough for amateur trekkers, family outings, a stroll in the park or a jog. There is a main building for meeting area, car drop off and pick up, giant ceiling fans to cool you down, chilled drinking water dispenser, vending machines, maps, loo and a park ranger on duty.  Good place to shelter if it rains There are some slopes on the way up to the cliff top of the old quarry with a stunning viewing Birds Eye area perched right at the top of the cliff. Not a long distance but short slightly steep slopes but totally worth the climb. Saw families with kids around the age of 5 and above at the top so it’s really not that challenging or difficult. Also Not as far as I expected. But do take note of your way back because if you take a wrong turn and end up on Rifle Range Road it’s more than a 1 hour walk to the nearest MRT station especially if you are already tired. However the paths are all well paved and safe. Do note the park closes at 7pm. You can also access this park from the Green Corridor.

About mid range level of difficulty. Lot of monkey though. Place is very peaceful and the lookout area is scenic. In awe. It’s connected to Rail Corridor as well, that one is pretty easy in difficulty level. It’s a literally straight road. Just need endurance for the distance.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026

After the ABC active beautiful clean PUB upgrading for 5 years. Finally there is peace and greenery. More animals and diff plants.
And chickens could fly high up the trees till level 2 -3. Gallery 18/18.
----
There are more variety of birds but the squirrels disappeared.

A pair of cats between Blk 607 and 613.

The water stream, reflection and sunset is beautiful.
Simply Beautiful & Tranquil.
Nice green space by the canal with plenty of benches to sit and relax.

🚤⛵🛳️This Promenade Lookout Jetty with a sweeping view of the stunning shoreline, often moored with ocean-going ships, small colourful fishing boats, stunning yachts and even Jackup Oil Rigs is a popular spot for fishing enthusiast, as well as a great spot to view the sunset.
🏝️West Coast Park which was built on reclaimed land was experiencing coastal erosion, in the early days.
📥To alleviate the erosion at the shoreline, a granite barrier, along with a picturesque promenade was built.
🎣🛟A Lookout Jetty was also included along with the scenic Promenade.

The Promenade Lookout Jetty at West Coast Park is a hidden gem offering a panoramic coastal view that stretches from the Republic of Singapore Yacht Club to Pasir Panjang Terminal and Jurong Island. It's a perfect spot for watching sunsets, with the serene atmosphere enhancing the experience. Photographers will appreciate the expansive views and peaceful surroundings, making it an ideal location for capturing stunning waterfront scenes. The jetty is rarely crowded, providing a tranquil escape where you can relax and take in the beauty of the coast.

Good:

1. A small quiet pier at West Coast Park.
2. Good view of Singapore Port.
3. Golden sunsets on non cloudy days..
4. Police cctv camera give a sense of security.
5. Police patrol duty every  night is very reassuring.
6. Great opportunity for photo ops and drone videography.

Bad:

1. Not a scenic beach. The view in the front is spoiled by cranes and ships.
2. Jetty is very short. Doesn't take you far into the sea.
3. Not ample shelter around. Need to run in case of sudden showers / thunder storms.
4. Do not go down into the water unless an expert swimmer. It is deceptively deep esp during high tide.
5. Lower rocks are exposed during low tide. Don't go down as these can be as slippery as ice.

It is a small spot inside the west coast park, where you can sit on a bench and look the harbour. You nearly have the same view as along the normal border path (along the harbour and on the side of the playground area). In the first picture: how you reach the spot. In the second one: the view
My first time to see super boat on the port. The area fits for circling and running, pinic also a good choice

Waterfront Promenade is exactly what you hope it will be — a relaxed, scenic walkway along Marina Bay that’s perfect for a leisurely walk, people-watching, or taking in the city’s iconic skyline.
We started our walk in the late afternoon and stayed until after sunset. The path is wide and well-maintained, with plenty of places to pause and enjoy the views of Marina Bay Sands, the Singapore Flyer, and the sparkling lights of the city reflected in the water. There are plenty of benches and shaded spots, which made it easy to take a break and soak up the atmosphere.
What really stood out was how peaceful it felt even when there were other people around. It’s busy enough to feel lively, but never too crowded to enjoy your own space. There are food and drink options nearby if you want a refreshment, but we also saw many locals and visitors bringing their own snacks and just enjoying the waterfront vibe.
The promenade is great at any time of day — sunny mornings, golden hour before dusk, or in the evening when the city lights come on. Whether you’re a visitor exploring Marina Bay or a local out for a casual walk, it’s a beautiful, easy-going stretch that captures that quintessential Singapore waterfront experience.
Highly recommended for a relaxed outing and especially good for families, couples, or anyone who loves great views with minimal fuss.

The Waterfront Promenade is a peaceful escape in the middle of the city. With its wide walkways, calming sea breeze, and stunning skyline views, it’s perfect for slow walks, quiet reflection, or late-night strolls. The atmosphere feels cinematic—especially at sunset and at night when the city lights reflect on the water.
It’s not crowded like other tourist spots, which makes it ideal for solo time, journaling, or simply letting your thoughts wander. Whether you’re healing, thinking, or just enjoying your own company, the promenade offers a calm, grounding energy that stays with you even after you leave.

🌃✨ Strolling along here at night felt like walking into a postcard! The skyline lit up, the reflections on the water, and the cool breeze made it such a dreamy spot. Totally worth the walk and definitely one of those must-do experiences when you’re in the city!

One of my absolute favorite spots in Singapore to simply relax and enjoy the moment. You get an amazing view of the Marina, and it’s the perfect place to slow down and take in the city. There’s often live music or street performances, and in the evening you can watch the lights of the business district slowly turn on — such a beautiful and calming sight.

A wonderful place to unwind, people-watch, and soak in the Singapore vibes.

The Waterfront Promenade in Singapore is a vibrant blend of urban design and natural beauty, offering visitors a scenic space perfect for leisure and relaxation. Stretching along Marina Bay, it provides panoramic views of the city skyline and iconic landmarks like the Marina Bay Sands and ArtScience Museum. The pathway is ideal for joggers, cyclists, and those looking to take leisurely strolls, especially at sunset when the bay is bathed in warm hues. The promenade’s highlights include artistic installations and lookout points that enhance the experience, while food and drink options nearby cater to different tastes. In the evening, the space comes alive with lights reflecting off the water and occasional events or light shows that add to the allure. Overall, the Waterfront Promenade is a must-visit for both tourists and locals seeking a refreshing, open-air experience amidst Singapore’s bustling cityscape.

Omg such a really nice place to visit. Its classy. The building ng has all the luxury stores from a to z worth millions of dollars. Very neatly maintained. Great tourist spot. Highly recommended place for everyone.

I’ve been there many times and I still never get bored. I love the vibe, whenever I visit Singapore, I know I’ll be walking a lot

It’s quite similar to Jakarta in terms of public transport, everything connects to tourist attractions and busy areas, but Singapore is way more organized

People and vehicles are very disciplined, there’s traffic sometimes, but not as chaotic as Jakarta LOL

Honestly, I’ll probably never get tired of going to Singapore...

Marina Bay Waterfront is easily one of the most breathtaking places to visit in Singapore. The moment you arrive, you’re greeted by a stunning panoramic view of the city skyline, with iconic skyscrapers rising elegantly against the sky, especially magical as the sun begins to set and the city lights slowly come alive.

The promenade itself is impeccably clean and well maintained, making it a pleasure to walk along. Every step feels comfortable and safe, whether you’re taking a casual stroll, jogging, or simply enjoying the fresh air. Along the way, you’ll find a wide variety of restaurants and cafés, offering everything from quick bites to fine dining with incredible views.

What truly stands out is how many beautiful photo spots are scattered throughout the area. Whether it’s the skyline reflection on the water, modern architectural landmarks, or perfectly landscaped corners, it’s a paradise for photography lovers.

There’s also a spacious garden area where you can sit back, relax, and soak in the atmosphere.

Overall, Marina Bay Waterfront perfectly blends urban sophistication with natural beauty. It’s a must-visit spot whether you’re a tourist or a local looking for a peaceful yet visually stunning escape

Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade -
A Symphony of Light, Architecture, and Heartfelt Memories: An Unforgettable Experience at Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade

My recent visit to Singapore was filled with incredible sights, but nothing captured the soul of this modern city-state quite like my evenings spent walking along the Marina Bay Waterfront Promenade. It is not merely a path; it is an experience, a masterpiece of urban design, and a testament to Singapore’s vision of blending nature with concrete

. I am writing this with immense gratitude to the planners, maintenance teams, and the city of Singapore for maintaining such a world-class, clean, and awe-inspiring space.

The First Impression: A Grand Promenade
From the moment I stepped onto the wide, impeccably maintained promenade, I was struck by the shear scale and beauty of the area. Stretching 3.5 kilometers around the bay, the promenade serves as a "grand front porch", linking major attractions like the Helix Bridge, Marina Bay Sands, and the Esplanade—Theatres on the Bay.

The attention to detail is remarkable; the flooring is comfortable for long walks, and there is a perfect balance between open spaces and shaded areas to escape the tropical heat.
Breathtaking Views and Architectural Marvels

The panoramic views from the promenade are absolutely surreal. Walking along the water, I felt surrounded by architectural masterpieces. On one side, the iconic Marina Bay Sands hotel towers with its awe-inspiring rooftop Skypark, while on the other, the futuristic dome of the ArtScience Museum resembles a lotus flower opening, perfectly framed by the skyline of the Central Business District.

I highly recommend visiting in the late afternoon, as I did. It allowed me to see the skyline transition from the bright, sunny afternoon into the "golden hour" before dusk, when the city lights begin to shimmer. The way the lights from the skyscrapers reflect on the calm water is a sight I will treasure forever—it’s like a postcard come to life.

The Magic of the Evening: Light, Sound, and Atmosphere
As the sun sets, the promenade transforms into a lively yet tranquil oasis. The atmosphere is vibrant, filled with people laughing, running, and capturing photos. I was particularly impressed by how the atmosphere changes from the bustling, lively sections near the dining options to the more serene, reflective stretches.

A heartfelt thank you to the organizers of the Spectra Light and Water Show. Watching the laser lights and dancing fountains projected to music right in front of the Shoppes at Marina Bay Sands was a highlight of my trip. It is a stunning, free, and accessible experience that showcases the city's commitment to public enjoyment.
A Sensory Experience: Mist Walk and Greenery

The promenade is designed with thoughtful features that make the walk incredibly enjoyable. I specifically loved the Mist Walk—a 250-meter long metal structure that emits a refreshing mist of water. It was a wonderful, creative way to cool down while strolling along the waterfront. Furthermore, the landscaping, with various trees and flowering shrubs, provides a natural feel that softens the surrounding modern skyline.

I visited Marina Bay Sands around golden hour and it was an amazing experience. The warm sunlight on the buildings and the reflection on the bay made the whole place look magical. The skyline view is incredible and the area is perfect for relaxing, walking, and taking photos.

Took a peaceful walk along the Tanjong Rhu Promenade Park Connector, enjoying the cool breeze and breathtaking views of the city skyline. The waterfront pathway, with its calm surroundings and scenic beauty, made for a perfect escape from the usual hustle and bustle.

From the reflections of Marina Bay Sands on the water to the quiet charm of the National Stadium in the distance, every step along the promenade felt refreshing. Definitely a great place for a relaxing walk, jog, or just to soak in the tranquil vibes of Singapore’s urban nature.

Really nice in the evenings, as many residents and people around the east, come to exercise and let out some steam. If it's not raining, the weather can be very breezy, and you can be sure to expect many dogs, bikes, and fitspo humans. Good for walks and strolls too!

Nice morning walk with easy access via Stadium station (CC6) or Tanjong Rhu station (TE23).
Multi connecting options to East Coast, Marina Barrage, Garden by the bay, etc...

A nice place to chill and walk along the kallang waters when the sun is settling and theres a nice cool breeze..def bustling with park goers and activities unlike many other park connectors.. head there for tranquility and to take in beautiful sights
Wonderful place to walk on wkend. There no shelter along the way though.

place_name: KidsCampus Jurong West Childcare & Infant Care
review_text:
Our hearts are truly overflowing with gratitude. For the past six years, KidsCampus Jurong West has been more than just a preschool. It has been a second home to my children.
To every teacher who has walked alongside them, thank you for caring with such genuine empathy and unwavering passion. Your ability to understand their feelings, comfort them on tough days and celebrate their little victories means more to us than words can express.
Your passion for teaching shines through in every moment such as your creative lessons, your joyful energy and the way you inspire the children to explore, imagine and believe in themselves. Because of all of you, our kids run into school with bright smiles each day, knowing they are safe, loved and truly understood.
Under your nurturing and heartfelt guidance, they have grown, thrived and blossomed in ways we could only hope for. We will forever be grateful for your compassion, your dedication and the incredible impact you have had on their early years.
Thank you, KidsCampus Jurong West, for being such a beautiful part of our children’s journey. ❤️

place_name: KidsCampus Jurong West Childcare & Infant Care
review_text:
Kids Campus has been amazing for our child.

The teachers are incredibly attentive, and the centre offers a wide range of engaging activities, which they update weekly through the Little Room app. The overall atmosphere is warm, positive, and nurturing.

I’m very impressed by how much effort the teachers put into the year-end concert. Everything was so well-planned and thoughtfully executed. It’s clear they’re giving their best to create a memorable experience for both children and parents.

We’re truly grateful to the Kids Campus team for their dedication and hard work.

Thank you, Ms.Kelly and all the teachers!
