review_text: Ms. Kelly attended to my enquiry regarding the infant class promptly and professionally. The teachers at the infant care are truly exceptional—caring, nurturing, and dedicated to the children’s well-being. Shayden enjoys going to school every day and often refuses to go home because he feels so happy and comfortable there. I would like to thank all infant teachers (Tr. Fadila, Nicole Laoshi, Zheng Laoshi & Wang Laoshi) for making each day enjoyable and meaningful for him. I truly appreciate their warmth and commitment in creating such a positive environment for Shayden.

place_name: KidsCampus Jurong West Childcare & Infant Care
review_text: We are very thankful for this wonderful kindergarten. The teachers are kind, caring, and dedicated, and they create a safe and happy learning environment. We’ve seen great growth in our child and truly appreciate all the love and effort the staff puts in every day.

place_name: KidsCampus Jurong West Childcare & Infant Care
review_text:
Dear KidsCampus Teachers & Staff,

Thanks for the wonderful memories and experiences during Haadif’s 18 months there. He learned a lot and bonded well with the teachers.

As a parent, I’m thankful for the amazing dedication & efforts by the infant teachers. Any questions or concerns were addressed professionally and I love how we can work well despite worrying much about our child. He was cared for carefully during his time at KidsCampus ❤️

This Bus stop, located at the very busy West Coast Highway, is the most convenient access to the lower (east) part of the West Coast Park. After crossing the overhead bridge the greenery immediately take over and the feelings change. It is also the best stop for pedestrians to reach the Republic Yacht Club.
I like this bus stop. It has all the buses i need. Could have more buses however.

West Coast Plaza is a pleasant small-to-medium sized neighbourhood mall with a good mix of restaurants, cafes, enrichment centres, retail shops, and everyday essentials. It has a relaxed atmosphere compared to the larger malls in Singapore, which makes it comfortable for casual shopping and meals

The mall is especially convenient for nearby residents, with useful tenants like Cold Storage, Anytime Fitness, Starbucks, Toast Box, and various dining options spread across the different levels. There are also quite a number of enrichment and tuition centres, so the mall feels family-oriented and community-focused

The layout is spacious and relatively easy to navigate, although some parts of the mall can feel a little quiet during off-peak hours

Quaint shopping mall in the neighbourhood. One of my most frequented malls in Singapore. Overall a pretty well-rounded shopping mall with many shopping options like Mr DIY, Daiso, Puma, Cold storage etc. While this shopping mall does not have a Macdonald or food court, there is a subway, and a newly-opened Popeyes. Those looking for cheap bubble tea can go for Mixue, or Fun toast and Toast Box if they are craving for coffee. There are also claw machines in a corner if you are into that kind of thing.

My daughter goes to Mindchamps and learns Taekwondo at Ildo at West Coast Plaza, so we frequent it every now and then. I love the Cold Storage, Starbucks, Puma, Wine Connection, Sanook, and other shops. It's a good and quick way to unwind and relax with some retail therapy.

They also have Candyland, which is my daughter's local favorite indoor playground.

It is a convenient and well-rounded mall, especially for residents living nearby. It offers a good mix of facilities, including dining options, toy stores, pharmacies, tuition centres, and gyms, making it suitable for everyday needs. The mall is usually quite lively, with regular events such as sales promotions and small activity setups in the central area, sometimes including playgrounds for children. Overall, it has an active and family-friendly atmosphere, making it a practical and enjoyable place to visit.

A quiet mall frequented mostly by locals. Has cold storage with good wine collection, two wine shops, Korean provision shops, parlours and eateries. Prices are mid level. Good for small kids for its indoor (paid) play area.

Refurbished recently. Some nice deco outside but not much has changed.
The net playground is still there, which is a good thing and a unique attraction to this mall.

🟠 City Square Mall
180 Kitchener Road
Singapore 208539

🟣 Location =  🟣 the mall is located at the junction of Serangoon Road and Kitchener Road.

🟣 It is directly connected to the Farrer Park MRT station via B1 level passage-way.
______________________________________________
⬛️  Historical Context: The mall actually sits on the former site of the New World Amusement Park, which was a famous landmark in the Jalan Besar area for decades.

⬛️  When I was a child in the early 1970s, my late Peranakan father used to bring me to New World to jalan jalan.
________________________________________________
🟠 The mall had undergone a SGD50 million massive revamp from September 2023 to early 2026.

🟠  The revamp added 26,000 sq ft of space, featuring a new 24,000 sq ft "Gastro Square" Level 4 dining area, new playgrounds, and classy luxurious interior upgrades.

🟨 The perimeters ( outside stretch of the mall)
*************************************************
🟨  There is a small verdant green park at the back of the mall, where a decomissioned MRT train cabin sits. ▪︎It is a hub for educational purposes.

🟨 There is also a small Merry-Go-Round & a Tea Cup Spinning Ride, reminiscent of the rides I used to enjoy in the 1970s.

🟩 For the interior of the mall itself
************************************
➡️ Below Info ➡️  is from the lift directory panel.

🟩L1 = City Green

🟩 B1 = Underpass to MRT, Taxi Stand, Pick-Up & Drop-Off, Green Square/ NTUC Fairprice/ Q&M Medical & Dental

🟩 B2=  ATM / Customer Service Counter/ Don Donki

🟩 B3=  Car Park + Food Square + Food Republic

🟩 B4= Car Park

⬛️ L9 To L6 = Schools & Offices  + Singapore Sustainability Academy (SSA)

⬛️ L5 = Discovery Square & Golden Village Cinema

⬛️ L4=  Gastro Square & CONNECT @ City Square Mall ( 🔴5 social service agencies)

⬛️ L 3 =  MST Golf Super Store

⬛️ L2 =  Decathlon & Uniqlo
________________________________________
🔴 Important info:
*******************
🔴 Level 4 features a CONNECT at City Square Mall, a dedicated community space comprising 5 ( five) social service agencies, affiliated with NCSS ( ▪︎National Council of Social Services), which itself is a statutory board under MSF.

🔴 MSF = Ministry of Social and Family Development.

🔴 The five agencies on Level 4 are
**************************************
● Counselling and Care Centre
● Kampong Kapor Community Services
● Singapore Children’s Society
● The Salvation Army

● Eagles Mediation & Counselling Centre ➡️ at B2 level

➖️ Dated 24 March 2026/ Tuesday.

If you are around Little India area, City Square Mall is actually a pretty nice place to chill and makan.
It's just a short walk from Mustafa Centre and the mall has a good mix of food spots, from quick bites to proper restaurants and food court options. Halal food choices are quite easy to find too.

Shopping wise, it's the kind of mall that feels practical and comfortable. Got everyday stores like Uniqlo and Decathlon, so you can actually shop without everything feeling overly expensive.

Bonus point for tea lovers… theres Beutea too🧋

It’s a nice mall and came here because to buy a drink from the nice supermarket. There is a Rest n Go area where there are many massage chairs. You have to scan the QR code to make the payment first and select which package. It’s worth as paying for $15 and 70mins of relaxation! Even paying $10 and 45mins of relaxation is good too. There were many willing to pay and relax on the chairs here and that’s good.

Latest update! A wonderful discovery and if you’re looking for a barber nearby this mall, there’s one! Jerudili Barber and it’s operated by a passionate with professionalism guy. He takes care of your hair well and really comfortable. He cuts hair great too! Please do support since it’s quite new. It’s at 681 Race Course Road and exit F, once alighted from Farrer Park MRT station. I have attached the barber shop photo too which includes the address.

I was here recently and went up to the highest level to explore. Seems like they have a good place for someone to hold a small seminar here but in a public place that is quite exposed to other sounds. But useful nevertheless. Not seen in this short video but here.

While waiting for appointment time for lunch and meeting up with friends on a Thursday afternoon, I shot this video.

This is a market you literally visit in the middle of the night. By the time we arrived around 2 a.m., many items were already sold out and we were picking through the scraps.

That said, it's an experience you should try at least once. We scored 5 huge tiger prawns for just $40, which is incredible value considering they'd likely cost $20–$30 per piece in a restaurant. You'll see many traditional fish vendors selling their fresh catch straight from the ships.

Just be mindful that the primary audience here are fish mongers supplying traditional wet market stalls, so navigate accordingly.

Note: bring your Singpass, to get through security.
Not the usual touristy place, but highly recommend it for offbeat Singapore experience. Great for taking pictures also.
Wide arrays of seafood, ranging from fishes to prawns to squids. Prices are better than what you'll normally purchased from  supermarkets or markets.
Nice experience.... Lots of fresh Fishes...

Come here only after 10pm to get your fresh fish and prawns!
No cutting services though. Be prepared to cut and gut your own purchase.
Drive in or get a rental car. Or just Grab there.

Went to the outlet (Tagore) to place an order for CNY, to make sure that delivery would be in place for reunion dinner.

Food was delivered just before the scheduled delivery time, which was exactly what we needed. Quality was good and tasted fresh. The fishballs and beef balls were especially well received.

Will use them again next time we do a steamboat.

What a great wet market, combined with Sheng Siong, lots of independent small stores, food court. The prices are fantastic compare to NTUC Fair price at the wet market. There’s heap of hawker food too, especially busy were prata, chicken rice, Lor mee and bee hoon and curry chicken. Nice atmosphere in the weekend and old nostalgia where your ah-Ma would love to shop here. Just wished it had more meat and vegetable stores in the wet market section. Good place to show the kids and spend the morning in the weekend.

I highly recommend this butcher!  Prime Pork ribs are very high quality, smooth, tender and fresh.  Their minced pork is the perfect meat/ fat ratio, very fresh.  No rancid smell at all.  I’ve also purchased their deboned chicken (quarter) 500gm pack many times- used it for grilling, steaming, braising.  Also recommend their pork meat balls- very tasty, has cuttlefish in it.  They deliver the items in an insulated box, full of ice packs to keep the meat cold and safe.  Everything is vacuum packed.  I’m so pleased with their service.  Their pricing is also competitive.  The thing with going to the wet market physically, you still need to repack into your desired portions upon coming home, so ordering from quan shui really saves me a lot of time and energy.

I always love going to the wet markets but cannot afford the time to wake up super early to get the freshest produce! Love that they deliver to your doorstep and even give the option to recycle the ice packs/boxes. The selection of produce is great, quality is really good and worth every bit of the slight premium!! Fruits & meat are super fresh! Ready to eat section is great for quick meals too! 100% will recommend!

We cooked with the wild caught balai threadfin for porridge, Silverfern short rib shabu shabu for Korean beef Bulgogi, silverfern ribeye steak for Beef bowl. It was amazing! 👌

We’ve been buying from Quan Shui Wet Market for a few times, and each time a satisfying one. Their items are fresh and clean, reached us in good condition and packaging. Complimentary delivery is reliable and good! Joyce from customer service is friendly and helpful. So far we’ve purchased almost all types of what they are selling and we’ll still purchase from them for long. Thank you Quan Shui Market!

Anytime Fitness West Coast Plaza has a spacious layout with a good variety of equipment for both strength and cardio workouts. There are plenty of machines, free weights, and functional training areas, so it generally doesn’t feel too cramped even during busier periods. The gym also has the usual Anytime Fitness convenience of 24/7 access

However, cleanliness and maintenance could definitely be improved. Some machines looked dusty, there were areas with visible litter and overflowing bins, and overall housekeeping did not feel up to the standard expected for a premium gym membership. A few parts of the equipment also appeared worn out and could use better upkeep

It's a decently sized space, considering how everything is so cramped in Singapore. The place is in need of a revamp, though. The functional area and studio are huge and could be put to better use.

I'm happy because I can be caveman and do my unga bunga lifts, but would be nice if the plates didn't have a sticky residue on them. A good clean and refreshed equipment would be neat.

Some machines are too faulty to be adjusted. Tried adjusting the machines to fit my smaller body proportions but got cut in the process. Leg curl machine cushion has been replaced with a matt since i joined in jan and could have been there for quite long already? Hope the machines can be replaced soon 🙏🏻

The gym floor is spacious after renovation. It is more powerlifting friendly - 3 deadlift platforms, 3 squat racks. Couldn't say the same for the plates which are aging and worn. Plenty of machines for legs like hip thrust, hack squats. Too bad most of the gym-goera here couldn't appreciate this. They have a spacious functional training area and group classes too. There are plenty of shower stalls here as well. This would be an ideal outlet for me if it wasn't for the distance (i live in Yishun).

Anytime Fitness at West Coast Plaza is a modern, well-equipped gym. Accessible 24/7, this branch is particularly spacious.
With a wide range of cardio and strength training equipment, a functional training area, free weights, and spaces for class.
The gym's atmosphere is conducive and well maintained. Overall a clean, spacious environment for users in the area.

First time at EagleWings Cinematics, after having a great dinner at EagleWings Loft a few months back.

First impressions were excellent. The staff warmly showed us around the lounge and explained how we could enjoy the place before the movie. We were especially excited to order the lobster risotto again, which we had been craving.

The lounge experience was affected when a rowdy group arrived before what seemed to be a company movie screening. Not the cinema’s fault, but it would help if staff could gently guide such groups to available seats instead of allowing them to block walkways and inconvenience other guests.

Our sides arrived quickly, though the risotto took a little longer. Staff were attentive throughout and checked with us on whether we preferred to dine in the lounge or in the hall.

The Platinum Hall was beautifully furnished and comfortable. Our food was neatly arranged at our seats when we entered. One thing to note is that the top-row “sofa” seats do not have centre armrests, unlike the lower rows, which may matter depending on seating preference.

Before the movie started, a family of three occupied two sofa sections, and the child was climbing around and making quite a bit of noise. Thankfully, it stopped once the movie began after the finally parents stepped in. It did make me wonder whether there are checks to ensure guests are seated according to their bookings.

Overall, it was still a very enjoyable evening, and I would gladly return again, hopefully without another large corporate screening crowd while in the lounge, or rowdy children in the theatre.

Made a trip today to try out EagleWings Cinematics for the first time, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. While we initially encountered some hiccups with the lounge service, the manager and team were proactive, sincere, and prompt in addressing our concerns, which we truly appreciated. The theatre itself was well-maintained, with spacious and comfortable seating. Dining in the theatre was enjoyable, and the Loft Menu offered good food and snacks at reasonable prices. Given the many positive aspects of our visit, we look forward to returning.

Everything was good but the service of the food counter staff on duty today. We ordered popcorn from the QR code in our seats, but after 15-20 mins it still had not arrived. We went out and saw that your staff Madison who was at the popcorn counter was playing with her phone and did not take notice of our order. Re-training highly encouraged.

Amazing experience. We booked in advance the premium lounge. My son and I used their massage chairs. That alone was worth the experience. It was like getting a deep tissue massage. They seem to have upgraded their massage chairs recently. Oh and free flowing drinks (hot or hold). (I recommended u come at least 45mins before the cinema start time). Then we went in, each two people can sit together on a couch which reclines and allows you to stretch your legs. It even comes with a fresh blanket in a pouch. Then u can scan the QR code and order as if you’re at a restaurant with a garment menu. Optionally you can also order popcorn 🍿and pay as u order. or press a separate call button to order more free soft drinks.

Royal experience. We watched supermario galaxy movie and the sound system and the screen size was suitable for the size of the room.

I realized that we picked the best cinema in SG when we kept talking about how awesome the experience for 10mins while we walked back to the MRT. We started planning our next visit as soon as we walked out.

Oh and the kindness and the hospitality is something else. This great level of treatment of customers is a rare treat in Singapore.

I wish I can give it higher than 5 stars
⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ it’s obvious whoever came up with this idea loves cinema, food and wants to share their passion with other people in SG. I wish this place more success and expansion in SG.

Kitty and I have been loyal patrons of EagleWings Cinematics for years, and we absolutely adore this cinema.  It's a truly unique experience that we keep coming back to again and again.

EagleWings Cinematics is located within the KAP Mall, and it stands out from other cinemas in Singapore because of its intimate and luxurious atmosphere.  Each theater is cozy and designed to hold a small group of people, like 12 to 20 people, making it feel like a private screening. It's so comfortable and relaxing!  The seating is fantastic, and we've tried all their classes—Silver, Gold, and Platinum.  Our favorites are Gold and Platinum, which offer plush reclining sofas with blankets to keep you warm.

But the real highlight is their amazing service and amenities.  Right next to the cinema is a fantastic restaurant where you can order a wide variety of food, from local favorites like laksa to international dishes like steak and fish and chips.  You can even order beer if you choose Gold or Platinum class.  It's a far cry from the typical cinema snacks! You can also enjoy their lounge one hour before your movie starts, with free-flowing coffee and soft drinks, and even two massage chairs to relax in!

The service is impeccable.  They efficiently deliver your food to your table in the theater, and the table lamps are perfectly positioned to illuminate your meal without disturbing other guests.  The sound system is excellent, and the air conditioning is just right.  It's a comfortable and enjoyable movie-going experience.

We love how EagleWings never feels crowded or rowdy.  The atmosphere is always so peaceful and relaxing, perfect for enjoying a movie.  We've recommended EagleWings to many of our friends, and they've all become addicted to this amazing cinema.  From that point on, they've been watching movies at EagleWings exclusively!

The overall experience is always positive.  It gives you a holistic experience, where you can enjoy your movie on the comfortable sofa seat and have delicious meals in the cinema.  The food we've tried over the years has always been above average.

EagleWings is also incredibly convenient.  All the theaters are on level 1, and if you're looking to book a private function, you can head to level 2.

We highly recommend EagleWings Cinematics to anyone looking for a luxurious and memorable cinema experience.  It's a great option for a romantic date night, a family outing, or a night out with friends.

A beautiful, relaxing and vibrant place for walks, for taking a step back from the hectic pace.
Spending 3 hours walking and appreciating the fresh air, the manicured gardens, the lake and wonderful architecture, helped to destress and reset.
If you've not been, well worth some time out.

Definitely one of the nicest and must go to parks in Singapore if you are alone, couples, families group of friends wanting to escape from the concrete jungle of the city. Jurong used to be perceived as a very faraway venue to commute to but with so many new MRT train lines it’s so much easier and faster to reach. Do take note of opening hours. Free entry and easily accessible with many entrances by Chinese Garden MRT or Lakeside MRT and buses. Great for all activities, walking , strolling, jogging, cycling, picnics and there is even a skate park and dog run. What makes this park stand out from the rest is the sheer size, the large lake with canoeing; stunning replicas of historical Chinese and Japanese architecture and pagodas, bonsai garden and wildlife. And especially the stunning Lily Flower Pond at the Japanese Garden and the Lotus Flower Pond at the Chinese Garden. Definitely worth to make a trip down to enjoy this green oasis escapade rich in flora and fauna and water. It’s very tranquil and peaceful from Mondays to Saturdays. Ample toilets and drinking water dispenser and also a beverage shop selling nice cold beverages.

NParks really did a good job in revamping this park.
There's plenty of interesting plants and wildlife to spot.
Took me and my friends 3 hours to casually explore the entire area.
It's definitely a nice place to run in the mornings/evenings.

I highly recommend to visit this gardens, as there are a lot of attractions to see and they are all free! It’s a beautiful area to dive into culture and nature, just 40 min from Singapore with the bus. Very relaxing and a moment to take a pause from the overtourism and consumistic view of marina bay and the Singapore center.
Not as big as the botanic garden, but I’d say it’s better to view this than that.
Really nice gardens. Will be even more pleasant to walk in when the trees in the still new Japanese and Chinese gardens grow older and provide more shade. Looking forward to that!

We’ve had a wonderful experience with Smart Berriis @ WestCoast! The teachers are caring and attentive, and the curriculum balances learning and play perfectly. The environment is clean, safe, and welcoming. Our child has grown in confidence and social skills since joining. Highly recommend this center to any parent!

Thank you, Smart Berriis team, for your dedication and care!

Our youngest son joined the playgroup class in August 2024, and we could not be happier with his experience. Mrs. Fong, in particular, has been excellent. Her warmth and genuine love for children, and her extensive experience puts us completely at ease. We are also grateful for Ms. Elizabeth, who is also very caring and dedicated. As an educator myself, I deeply value their sacrifice, the long hours they put in, and the smiles they bring into the classroom. We are so thankful to have them guiding our youngest as he takes his first step in learning.

My daughter has a very very happy time in Smart berriis@West coast. She stays here for one year, half a year in N2 and the other half year in K1. She loves the teachers in N2 and K1 very very much. Thank Smart Berriis@West coast for giving her so much great time and so beautiful memory here.

I highly appreciate the concern the teachers and staff are giving to my son. I know that when I leave him at school, he is well taken care of and he is also enjoying. He is continuously learning a lot everyday and he’s growing a lot more independently and confidently.
My son has been at this school for 1.5 years now, and he's happy to go every day. The teachers are super dedicated, and thanks to them, he's really picked up discipline and independence.

Mr Kor is an excellent POA teacher who explains concepts clearly and in a way that is way easier to understand as compared to school POA teachers. He is patient, supportive, and always encourages his students to do their best, even his students were not as confident of themselves as much as Mr Kor was for them.  Because of his teaching, I became much more confident in POA and more comfortable answering questions. His lessons are engaging and well-structured, and I am very grateful for the guidance and dedication he has shown. To end off, i was a former student of Mr Kor, and before i attended his lessons i was a standard F9 POA student and had zero interest. However after taking on his tutions, my grades were improving bit by bit and by O levels 2024, I got a B3. Hence, highly recommended !

My son was struggling with his Principal of Account. Mr Kor was god-sent during his O level year. My son did very well for his POA, securing his sweet spot in Poly through EAE.
According to my son, Mr Kor was a fun, dedicated and helpful teacher. He makes POA never a dull subject. My son is able to grasp the concepts well with Mr Kor's explanations and guidance. This translates to confident to tackle the subject and improved result.

Mr Kor is a patient and dedicated tutor who has a great way of interacting with students. He understands my child’s learning needs and teaches with clarity. Within a month I could see my child’s interest & confidence in Math improving. Thanks Mr Kor!

Big thanks to Mr Kor, for your patience and support. My child went from failing math to achieving good grade within short few months under your guidance. Your encouragement and teaching made all the difference!! It's also not just about the grade, it’s about the confidence and interest in learning that he’s helped build!

1. my main challenges before tutoring sessions was really just my attitude towards poa, because for me when i truly like a subject i’d be very motivated to study it. however for POA to me it was already a boring subject, dealing with numbers isn’t something i’m most fond of so i already had a negative perception of poa. and even when i tried my best to understand poa, my foundation was weak since in sec 3 i barely paid attention in class and was busy chatting away, i didn’t have a solid foundation and thus when it got to the harder parts like financial statements i often struggled balancing always missing 20k-30k.
2. ⁠I worried most about my poa when N levels were nearing since i still was unstable to balance my financial statements and my theory was really a lost case, i found Mr Kor through my moms friend and when he came he always encouraged me that i’d do better than expected, though i had doubts since i always have been failing, it was rly nice to know that someone believed in me. I told him which areas i didn’t understand and he would curate worksheets for me which targeted those areas, though i grumbled a lot those practices that he made me do, drilled the knowledge into me and i was able to slowly get comfortable with poa
3. ⁠Strategies and techniques that i found helpful was the fact that he would take time and effort to custom make the worksheets and practices for me that targeted my weak spots. He often also laid out the exams format on what they would usually test first. and because he had so much experience and put so much effort into laying it out for me. it brought me a sense of comfort
4. ⁠When i took my poa prelims (though i didn’t do very well i just passed) i realised i could actually understand what the question was asking me, and knew what i actually knew and did not know. even though my theory was still pretty rabak i could answer them unlike before where i would just scribble a random answer.
Though i just passed i felt extremely happy since i had always been failing poa.
5. I would reccomend these sessions to others since I felt that Mr Kor had a lot of experience and even if u think u are the worst poa student, he will be able to push out a B3 for you. I obviously doubted at first and thought he was just pitying me and just saying out of politeness that i would get a B3. However as the sessions went on, i slowly gained more confidence since he could explain it clearly to me even repeating them many times so i would remember them. I felt that he is a very genuine person and of course an amazing teacher. He has made poa an enjoyable subject for me and has remarkable teaching skills. He has changed my perception of poa and i now see it as a subject that i enjoy not something i should despise

The ice cream and waffles are tasty! I had cookies monster and waffles. The service was also commendable. Good neighbourhood location too. Nice place for friends to hangout.Thanks to Yuai and Yuan Sheng for accommodating to my request. Cheers!

really enjoyed their gelato and waffles! the waffles were crisp at the edges but had a chewy centre, which made every bite really enjoyable 😋 the hojicha gelato had a strong tea fragrance without being too sweet, while the pistachio one was another good and safe choice.

for a sweet & savoury combination, you can even get their mentaiko tater tots that were freshly fried and went perfectly with the umami mentaiko sauce 🍟🥔

Crispy, golden waffle topped with a scoop of creamy ice cream—simple, delicious, and perfectly balanced. Warm meets cold in the best way. Definitely a must-try.

Great waffle café with a cozy vibe. Friendly service, and fair prices. Definitely worth a visit!
Great friendly and fast service. The waffle is nice but abit dry today. Anyways hoping for paddlepop flavour!!
really amazing one of a kind waffles and unique ice cream flavours and super conducive to chill or study  will come back again! 🍨

Stepping into the Omni-Theatre at Science Centre Singapore felt like entering a completely different world. This isn’t your usual cinema — the massive dome screen and immersive surround sound make you feel like you’re inside the movie, whether you’re flying through space or exploring nature. The visuals are incredibly sharp and engaging, and it’s a great mix of entertainment and learning for both kids and adults.
One big tip: visit on a weekday. That’s truly the best time to go. The crowd is much smaller, and it feels almost like you have the theatre to yourself, especially if you’re going with family. The experience is more relaxed, quieter, and way more enjoyable without the usual weekend rush.
Ticket prices are also very reasonable. Singaporeans and Permanent Residents pay around $8, while tourists pay about $14, which is excellent value considering how unique the experience is compared to a regular movie theatre.
What I also love is how convenient the location is. After the Omni-Theatre, you can easily spend more time nearby. KidsSTOP™ is perfect for younger children with hands-on learning and play, while Snow City is just next door if you want something fun and cooling. It’s easy to turn this into a full half-day or even full-day family outing.
Overall, the Omni-Theatre is a great place to slow down, learn something new, and enjoy quality time together. If you’re looking for something different to do in Singapore — especially on a weekday — this is definitely worth checking out.

Brought my kids here today. It was pleasant even though 20mins into the show, one fell asleep and the other one was looking forward to the “good part” as we were expecting a show about space. However it was more of a NASA showcase. Commented that she came to learn about space, not rockets 😅

Not all of the footage made use of the unique omni dome but this could be due to the film’s origin. Sound could be improved. We sat at the top near the exit. Because the back speakers were off, the show lacked impact. It was also warm as if the aircon was off.

Parking was full. Some cars had to park on the side road inside carpark. Understood from staff that this was unusual as it’s the last day of Robofest at Science Centre. Perhaps some traffic control is required for busy days such as today.

We’ll come again for other shows and hope that the management look into the above especially the show experience. Still think it’s a core memory opportunity for kids as it did for me when I visited the omni theatre back in my school days.

As part of SG60, there was a promotion to visit the science centre and the Omni theatre free.  It was a bit confusing though as although the ticket listed this two places as free, you would have to book separately for each movie slot.  I learned later that each admission ticket that day could book one movie.  However they opened up another queue for walk in admission.  And I believed anyone who used the walk in managed to go in.

I managed to watch all the films that day except for Stars Dreaming.  Animal Kingdom was the most interesting film with nice visuals and interesting facts.  Cities of the future was quite a boring one that covered a few cities around the world on the issues of environment sustainability.  Some audiences actually walked out of it.  Kitz the cat's Secret of the Space Station was the only film with a storyline and also made use of the Onimax effect to make you nauseous and dizzy in a few scenes.  It was an animation targeted at children although the theme can be quite dark.  It was still educational.  I believed it was a Korean production and some dialogues were quite awkward. Overall I would only recommend Animal Kingdom.

I found the dome screen rather dim though.  But all the pixels on the screen were working fine.  The images were not clear and the animal kingdom felt like a low Res film.  The theatre is digital 8k though according to the staff.

In terms of environment, you need to put up with talking children and eating.  Even the mother next to me kept using her hp and I need to tell her about it.  Later she started to provide narration to her child.  So be prepared to put up with the annoyances of these people.

There were a lot of stairs climbing up and down.  If you are mobility challenged you could seek help from the staff and gained access via a lift.

This theatre along with the science centre would be torn down for another new building.  Not sure would there be an Omni theatre in the future.

Long ago I watched other shows like Grand Canyon, Space walk and diving in the sea.  All were good documentaries.

Got vouchers and brought my kids (5, 10, 12 and 14yo) to watch "Ocean Paradise". The venue is very clean (no eating and drinking allowed), comfortable and has a magnificent dome screen. The movie length of 35min was perfect for my 5yo (not too long) but also not so short that the trip wasn't worth it for my 14yo. The images from this show were stunningly beautiful and the messaging was clear. Nothing scary whatsoever, which is great for my easily-scared kids, but still having a strong storyline to keep everyone engaged throughout. Volume of sounds were also not too loud (unlike movie theaters) but still clear. Do check out the highly rated Indian Food stall just outside the omnitheater, or adjourn to the Regale for some pasta (tts what we ate anyways) if spice is not an option. Bottom line: fantastic immersive movie experience for kids (and adults)!!

I think the omni is losing its touch. The documentary is very boring. People expect to feel " they are in the movie" and yet we only feel like it is looking at a documentary. A lot of people end up sleeping.
Playground in jungle but really nice done. For small and big ones. Space to rest, lots of shade but sad they closed a caffe on the top of the hill.
It was an OK playground for kids below 12 years old. It consisted of some local themes such as a giant Chempedak and saga seeds. Would be great if there is at least one F&B nearby.

COMO Adventure Grove is not just a place for kids’ activities and fun, but also a wonderful spot to enjoy nature. 🌳 For us, it turned out to be a great area for jogging and walking and refreshing, spacious, and relaxing. It’s a nice balance of play, exercise, and nature all in one place. Definitely worth a visit whether you’re here for activities or just to stay active. 🚶🏻‍♂️🏃🏻‍♂️✨

An interesting playground for older kids. A sand pit with a very large durian-shaped structure and a Saga seed themed sand filled play area. There are swings and interesting slides. But the downside is, it can get rather hot on sunny day.

An adventure playground area located within the beautiful Botanic Garden and specifically in the Gallop Extension section. Kids would love the options like sand pits, swings and slides which are quite extensive. While the walking path is sometime shady, this part of the playground is in the open so plenty of sun.

One of the largest playground for kids from 1 to 10 years old. Very beautifully designed and kids will love the playground. The two play items that stand out most is the Viking Ship and the Flying Fox. Do not miss out visiting this playground if you have kids!

A great place to bring the kids! There are many playground areas, equipment (swings, slides, climbing structures, etc) and sand areas to keep the children occupied. There are also play equipment for the younger kids and toddlers too!

Not to forget the huge green spaces to play sports and games and run around.

Very nice playground with good amount of shade. Well maintained park. Playground has wide range from age group 3-5, 6-12, and 12+. Big sand pit that is not dirty. Recommended time to visit after 5~5:30pm to avoid the heat. The playgrounds are behind Mcdonalds

🛝Which kid can resist a Pyramid Space Net!
🛝And all the more, with two Slides at different heights.
🤼Kids can have endless fun time playing Catching (local term for Tag) on the Pyramid Space Net and taking to the slides.
It's always the first facility I visit in West Coast Park, it upholds my childhood, and I'm glad that this facility is still here!

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Had a wonderful time at Ola Beach Club in Sentosa! The beachfront and poolside atmosphere makes it a perfect place to relax, unwind, and enjoy the island vibes.

A special thank you to Chef Vishnu for his warm hospitality and kind hosting. He made our visit even more enjoyable with his attentive service and welcoming attitude.

The food and drinks were great too and we especially enjoyed the pizza, refreshing juices, and delicious ice cream. Everything was fresh, tasty, and perfect for a sunny day by the beach.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for a relaxing beachside dining experience with good food, great service, and beautiful views. Looking forward to visiting again! 🌴☀️🍕🍦🥤

A nice beachside spot with a great view of the sea. The place is family-friendly and even has a pool for kids, which is a nice touch. The DJ plays good music and creates a fun atmosphere. Cocktails are okay, though they could be a bit better. Overall, a good place to relax by the beach.

Really good food and drinks. They have different plates…as share main or share starter. Drinks are really good. Also, amazing beach spot. They have a wee swimming pool if you don’t want to be on the beach. Finally, really good customer service!

My family and I have visited Ola Beach Club several times, and each visit has been truly wonderful and memorable. The food and drinks are consistently excellent, and the ambiance strikes the perfect balance between relaxing and fun.

It’s a fantastic place for both the young and the young at heart to unwind, bond, and spend quality time together. Friends can also enjoy meaningful catch-ups in a vibrant yet laid-back setting.

For the kids, the swimming pool, sand play area, and banana boat ride are definite highlights, making the experience even more enjoyable for the whole family. Totally recommend this place!
Food were pretty good, we enjoyed our food! The pacing of the dishes could be better, my children have almost finished their food before ours were served. We enjoyed the atmosphere and the service!
A lovely stretch of park connector with shady trees and beautiful flowers in bloom.
